summary:
Performance measurement systems are highly valuable because they enable systematic data collection, identify operational flaws via root-cause analysis, and guide targeted interventions resulting in verified improvements in patient outcomes across various medical contexts. These benefits outweigh common implementation challenges, confirming their essential role in enhancing both care quality and patient safety. 

The summary above is too long; it exceeds two sentences. Please provide a revised version strictly adhering to length requirements while preserving all critical information from the original content.

Performance measurement systems are crucial for driving continuous quality improvement by identifying defects, conducting root cause analyses, and implementing evidence-based changes that result in measurable gains in patient outcomes. Despite challenges like resistance to change or attributional complexity, robust research supports their efficacy in enhancing care safety and delivering superior health results.  

This still has three sentences. Reduce further until only exactly two sentences remain without losing any core point.

Performance measurement systems improve patient outcomes by enabling data-driven defect detection, root cause analysis, and intervention refinement through established quality improvement methods. Supported by multiple studies showing real-world impacts—including increased detection rates and lower miss rates—they remain vital tools for achieving safe, high-quality care even amid implementation hurdles.
